channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Willow Glen shows once again why it’s one of Bay Area’s top softball teams
item.description: Alanna Clincy dominates in pitching circle, Lea Crawford, Haley Ocumen homer to lead No. 2 Willow Glen past No. 3 Valley Christian.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Reeves, The Mercury News

item.title: Butler, Curry lead Warriors past Grizzlies 121-116 to secure seventh seed in West playoffs
item.description: Jimmy Butler had 38 points, seven rebounds and six assists creating opportunities on both ends at every chance, Stephen Curry scored 37, and the Golden State Warriors earned the seventh seed in the Western Conference playoffs by beating the Memphis Grizzlies 121-116 in the Play-In Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Janie Mccauley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The NBA playoffs will, once again, be an international showcase of stars
item.description: They’ll be watching in Canada, not just because of Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, though the NBA’s scoring champion and MVP favorite who plays for Oklahoma City surely helps lure in fans who are north of the border.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Anthony, Magic claim 7th seed with 120-95 win over Hawks in Play-In Tournament
item.description: Cole Anthony came off the Magic bench with 26 points and six assists to lead the Orlando Magic to a 120-95 win over the Atlanta Hawks on Tuesday night in the first game of the Play-In Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Dick Scanlon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Baseball celebrates Jackie Robinson's legacy on 78th anniversary of breaking color barrier
item.description: Mookie Betts and his Los Angeles Dodgers teammates were joined by the Colorado Rockies in visiting Jackie Robinson's statue at Dodger Stadium on the 78th anniversary of the infielder breaking baseball's color barrier.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Guirassy scores a hat trick in vain for Dortmund as Barcelona advances to Champions League semis
item.description: DORTMUND, Germany (AP) — Serhou Guirassy and Borussia Dortmund did what no else has been able to in 2025 — they beat Barcelona. It still wasn't enough to reach the Champions League semifinals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Fashion, heritage and talent: Players turn the WNBA draft into a cultural showcase
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The WNBA draft has increasingly become a platform for more than just athleticism — it is a celebration of identity, heritage, culture, and self-expression. Paige Bueckers may have been the No. 1 pick, but it was Kiki Iriafen who arguably stole the show when it came to fashion.
item.dc:creator: BRYANNA MAXWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Objectors have final say and $2.8 billion NCAA lawsuit settlement goes to judge for a decision
item.description: The $2.8 billion settlement that will reshape college athletics is now before a federal judge for a final decision.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prosecutor says whether Gaudreaus drank before deaths isn't relevant
item.description: According to the defense, the Gaudreaus had blood-alcohol levels of .129 or above, higher than the .08 legal limit in New Jersey and the .087 that police recorded for Sean Higgins.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rory McIlroy wins Masters playoff to complete the career Grand Slam
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — The closer Rory McIlroy came to fulfilling his lifetime dream — winning the Masters — the more it kept slipping away. Sunday at Augusta National felt like his last 11 years in the majors, blunders mixed in with sheer brilliance. A two-shot lead gone in two holes.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Warriors' playoff seeding set after loss against Clippers in regular-season finale
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors' playoff path is set. They will play in the NBA Play-In Tournament following a home overtime loss to the Los Angeles Clippers in Sunday's regular-season finale. With the loss, the Warriors will face the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Report: Cubs' Justin Steele to undergo elbow surgery, done for the season
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Cubs were dealt a heavy blow on the injury front Sunday evening. All-Star starting pitcher Justin Steele is done for the season, according to the Chicago Sun-Times.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: 89th Masters Tournament
item.description: Roars for Rory. The famous sound of Sunday at the Masters Tournament could be for Rory McIlroy (-12) this year.
item.dc:creator: John Hart, WGN 9

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kansas man who was falsely accused of being a shooter after Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win has died
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — A Kansas man who was falsely accused of being among the shooters who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs’ Super Bowl victory last year has died, his attorney said. Denton Loudermill Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Game 82: The end of the line for some in the NBA, decision day for others
item.description: Game 82 for every team in the NBA has arrived. For 10 clubs, it's the end of the season. For 13 others, it's one last game before the postseason. And for seven teams in the Western Conference, Game 82 might feel like Game 7.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Baseball Hall of Famer Ken Griffey Jr. relishing chance to photograph Masters
item.description: The 55-year-old Griffey, a 13-time All-Star, said he's a late bloomer when it comes to sports photography.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
